title: Fridge Tour with Paul Saladino MD (2024 Update)

chunks.title: Introduction to the Kitchen
chunks.summary: The speaker introduces their kitchen and shares a bit about their preferences for cutting boards and meat processing tools. They mention their wood cutting boards and a meat grinder that will be used later.
chunks.transcript: what's up guys welcome to my kitchen I'm at home in Costa Rica this is my fridge tour 2024 Edition come check it out so I got the wood cutting boards here I got the meat grinder that's going to come into play later remember the meat grinder but wood cutting boards I hate plastic cutting boards I never use them so I've only got wood cutting boards in my house and we go past my display of the Harden soil supplements and some of my newest exciting thing the lineage meat sticks into the fridge ready bye

chunks.title: Fresh Juices and Organic Ingredients
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses their love for fresh juices, specifically watermelon and mango puree, and their experiences with local organic farmers in Costa Rica. They highlight the importance of sourcing high-quality, glyphosate-free honey.
chunks.transcript: I actually didn't make it look that fancy for you guys but I'll give you a tour over here we've got the watermelon juice I Juiced this myself I get the Watermelons organic at a local market this is some of the best stuff you'll ever have I reuse glass bottles from our a goat farm so you'll see a lot of the things in here say Hortensia or aisco La Hortensia which is a local goat farm but this is not goat yogurt this is watermelon juice I drink a lot of juice these days I'm pretty active surfing a couple hours a day so I have seen on my blood work which I've talked about in the past that there's really no negative consequences to doing more carbohydrates for me and 300 plus grams of carbohydrates a day I feel great so fruit juice is one of the ways I get it do what works for you in terms of carbohydrates this is mango puree I also make that in the juicer got some local organic glyphosate free honey here uh one of the things I love about living in Costa Rica is there are awesome farmers markets that I can go to I can talk to the farmers I can ask them how the honey is raised how the bees are raised if there's anything around the honey with pesticides when I'm in the States this is actually a brand of honey that I can find that's certified glyphosate free there are very few brands of honey that are glyphosate free stay tuned because linage is going to be bringing you guys a glyphosate free honey very very soon

chunks.title: Raw Dairy Products
chunks.summary: Various raw dairy products are showcased, including goat milk, yogurt, and kefir. The speaker explains the differences between kefir and yogurt and advocates for raw dairy consumption, sharing their personal experience with lactose intolerance.
chunks.transcript: over here I've got a bunch of my goat milk products I've got raw goat milk I've got raw yogurt and raw cafir so the difference between cafir and yogurt is that cafir is fermented for 24 hours at a lower temperature around 78 degrees fahren yogurt is fermented for a shorter amount of time at higher temperature and they use slightly different cultures so couple of different fermented foods here on the top shelf I've got some raw local butter this is from cows and I much prefer raw butter to pasteurized butter obviously if you can't get raw milk or raw butter don't stress it look for a farm near you you can go to realmilk.com to find if there are farmers near you who do raw dairy and so over here I've got some Tallow I'm a huge fan of animal fats guys I obviously hate seed oils so no seed oils here bought some Capers the other day I was feeling a little crazy and I was like oh maybe I'll throw some Capers on my burger I haven't decided if I like them or not yet it's hard to find capers without a bunch of and then here I got some raw chicken that I feed to my dog Zach so yeah I'm a huge fan of raw dairy I feel much better when I'm drinking raw dairy that's the chime for the refrigerator because I've had it open too long I'll close it for a second I used to be lactose intolerant and realize that there are naturally occurring organisms in raw milk raw butter raw cheeses raw cfers that can change the way that our guts are populated and I really don't have any sensitivity to lactose now so if you are lactose intolerant do not dismay it is very possible that you can do milk if you can find a good source of a raw milk yogurt cafir cheese butter Etc if you're

chunks.title: Animal Fats and Raw Meats
chunks.summary: The segment covers the speaker's enthusiasm for animal fats, particularly raw butter and beef. They discuss sourcing their meat directly from farms and the benefits of grinding their own meats, including heart and liver.
chunks.transcript: really in a state where that's hard to find you can almost always find raw cheese back into the fridge I've got some ground heart here so you saw the grinder earlier guys I grind this myself this is raw ground heart I make it into burgers or I mix it with a ground beef I ground this beef myself as well it's kind of fun and satisfying to have a grinder I know all of the steps of my food from the beginning to the end we'll close the refrigerator again because it'll beep at me otherwise but I can use the stainless steel grinder right here and I can grind the meats myself that is meat from a cow that I got from a friend's Farm here in Costa Rica I was actually there when the cow was butchered I know how the cow was raised and so I get pieces of meat and then I grind them myself into Burger I've got some of steaks in here too which I'll show you as well before I get to the steaks I've got a cucumber cucumbers are fruit I will take the skin off this take the seeds and then cut it up into pieces and add it to the top of a burger or something gives a little texture a different flavor cantaloupe which I'll probably juice some apples here maybe in the yogurt or with some cafir maybe I'll juice the apples down here on this shelf I've got some skirt steak I've got some bones here that I will use to make bone broth in the back there's some liver that's thawing I've got a heart that's thawing because I will eventually run out of this ground heart and I'll grind more I've got like a a May or lemon over here that I will put maybe on the cucumber and these drawers I've got peepas which are coconuts I use these for fluids

chunks.title: Benefits of Fruit Juice
chunks.summary: The speaker provides insights on the benefits of fruit juice, debunking myths around fructose and emphasizing the positive health effects of various fruit juices, including orange and watermelon juice.
chunks.transcript: they're super incredible source of potasium good source of Manganese so I juice a lot of oranges 12 to 18 oranges a day I don't fear fruit juice guys um if you want to see we'll put a link in the video to me going to a farmers market in Costa Rica and I've spoken about this in the past there are so many studies with fruit juice showing that it's beneficial for humans and really zero studies that I'm ever aware of somebody put it in the comments if you're aware of any study showing that fruit juice is bad for humans like a real fruit juice but orange juice improves endothelial function orange juice decreases lipid peroxidation orange juice decreases oxidative stress in humans the studies on watermelon juice grape juice pomegranate juice all kinds of juices they're all beneficial for humans so don't fear fruit juice if you guys are fearful of fructose I've done many podcasts trying to dispel that do what works for you but I think fruit fruit juice honey these are healthy foods for humans you don't need to fear

chunks.title: Personal Dietary Experiences
chunks.summary: Reflecting on their dietary journey, the speaker details their transition from a carnivore to an animal-based diet and the impact on their autoimmune conditions. They share personal experiences with foods like tomatoes and the importance of finding what works for individual health.
chunks.transcript: these Foods especially in the whole food form and we'll go back to this one of the reasons I'm such a fan of heart and I actually do my own ground heart is I like the texture of the heart in a burger more than the regular heart and I like eating organs I eat liver about half an ounce a day and I eat heart probably three to four ounces of heart a day why do I like heart coenzyme Q10 torine riboflavin there are unique nutrients in heart that are not found in nearly the same amounts in muscle meat so I found that I feel the best when I combine some amount of organs with some amount of muscle meats and I'll grind my own heart like that and do that and in case you guys didn't know lineage meat sticks have liver and heart in them as well so there's all kinds of ways to get osmosis is just the easiest way that I have found to get the purest water obvious viously it takes everything out so I'm going to remineralize it with some microplastic free sea salt that's kind of a cool thing to have in your life or some Trace mineral drops but most of what I'm drinking now in fluids is the fruit juice or the orange juice and I'm getting extra water when I need it to stay hydrated I also use this water to cook with if I'm boiling something or if I'm making bone broth I will use the reverse osmosis water but realize guys that if you are drinking plastic bottled water that is a big source of microplastic some studies estimate five grams a week which sounds crazy to me maybe five grams a month that's the equivalent of a credit card in microplastics probably on the order of a month if you're drinking many bottles of plastic bottled water every day it's so much easier just to use something like a stainless steel water bottle for your water and avoid having your water touch plastic there was just a study that came out from the New England Journal of Medicine showing that in people who had more microplastics in their corroded plaque the plaque in their arteries they were four and a half times more likely to have cardiovascular disease level of their heart so microplastics appear to be inflammatory and damaging for Our arteries and I'm trying to minimize my exposure to them as much as possible which means no plastic cutting boards it means wood cutting boards it means I'm not drinking water out of plastic it means I'm not storing my food in plastic you saw no plastic that I could avoid soaring my food in that fridge everything is in glass that I've already touched yes there's some meat and plastic I'm not perfect it's impossible for me to be perfect but I'm doing the best I can but I'm thinking about it a lot and trying to minimize the microplastics in every way that I can I mentioned orange juice this is the best $10 I've ever spent this is a hand orange juicer nothing too fancy but I will juice The Oranges by hand every day with this little guy and it's great it's also great to travel with because you can make your own a plastic dish towel because or like a polyester fiber or a microfiber dish towel that's going to create more microplastics in the environment so I'm trying to get the natural fibers in in my kitchen I want to show you guys one more thing about the fridge before we wrap up this is the coolest part of the fridge you may have noticed that I have a bunch of really Hightech red plastic paper here it's not Hightech I'm joking this is because one of the things I hate is at night when I open the fridge and it's dark I have red lights in my house at night and this bright light from the fridge just trying to protect my C Rhythm as much as possible so that's my Kookie red light fridge for you guys you can tell all your friends about that one all right guys so that is a corner of my kitchen in Costa Rica I'll show you the whole house soon enough coming from a carnivore diet to an animal based diet opened up a lot of options for me and I noticed that when I go off animal-based diet often my chunks.title: Conclusion and Nutritional Advice
chunks.summary: In the concluding segment, the speaker shares broader nutritional advice, advocating for quality over quantity in food choices. They emphasize the importance of improving diet quality for health and offer suggestions for incorporating organ meats into diets.
chunks.transcript: calorie counting fails generally if you're trying to lose weight but improving the quality of your foods helps you stay full for longer this is what we call satiety so hopefully that fridge tour is helpful for you guys you can see what's in my fridge and Costa Rica if you find Value in this content I have a deal for you guys if you will hit the like button if you will subscribe to the content I will do more of this free content to help you on your health Journey thank you for your help with that guys and remember that if you need more organs in your life check us out at heart and soil .
